violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicers) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Proper sanitizing procedures reviewed with PIC. Slicers to be properly sanitized before use. New Violation. Correct By: 04/03/2025

Sink and surface sanitizer solution was in excess of (704-1875) parts per million (ppm). Sink and surface sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label. Establishment to dilute solution with water until company can adjust dispenser. New Violation. Correct By: 04/03/2025
Rodent droppings were observed on the bottom shelf of the soda storage area. Area to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 04/03/2025
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH. Application printed. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024

Chicken was observed thawing on the counter in stagnant water. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Chicken was moved to walk-in. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the slicer have an accumulation of food debris. The food debris was observed on the base of the slicer not on the blade. The non food contact surfaces of the shelving in the back had an accumulation of old rodent droppings. Establishment to pull all items off the shelf and wash rinse and sanitize shelf. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024
Old rodent droppings were observed underneath shelving in the back storage area. The facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024
